preamble: The shift from the bilabial nasal [m] to the labiodental fricative [v] is a hallmark of Celtic lenition (mutation). It involves the weakening of the bilabial stop closure into a fricative [β], which eventually loses its nasality and shifts its place of articulation slightly to become [v].

examples.note: The spelling 'mh' represents the historical lenited m.
Grammatical mutation of the initial nasal.
Soft mutation (treiglad meddal) shifts m to f [v].

sources: Thurneysen: A Grammar of Old Irish
